pages.raw_text:
The attendance at the meeting of
the Central Labor union last night
was the largest seen IN Spokane for
months and proves that the new
central body has met with favor
among local union men, and that in
terest is being taken in all matters
affecting organized labor by the
unions Of this city.


The cement workers affiliated
with the central body last night as
did federal union No. 222. The
plumbers and carpenters No. 1860
were represented by committees.
These unions will probably affiliate
with the central in the near future


Secretary McChesney reported
having received the new charter
from the American Federation of
Labor, but 1t came so late yester-
day that he had not had time to
have it placed in it's frame, but it
would be in its place by the next
meeting.
